UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GREGORY T. SUNDAY AND BEULAH SUNDAY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO (1) THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE ALL ELEMENTS OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION; (2) THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION TO RE-ALLEGE AS REMEDY AND/OR AS A PART OF COGNIZABLE CAUSE OF ACTION; (3) SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION TO RE-ALLEGE AS SEPARATE COGNIZABLE CAUSES OF ACTION. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT AS MEMORIALIZED IN THE JOINT APPLICATION FOR LOT ADJUSTMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE RONALD QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Carolyn Lee, Esq. of Tsao Chow & Yee, (415-777-1688), for Jinyuan Zhou, Plaintiff and opposing party.
No appearance by Defendants and moving parties.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS GREGORY T. SUNDAY and BEULAH SUNDAY's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. All causes of action are sufficiently alleged. Off Calendar Jan-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT GREGORY T. SUNDAY and BEULAH SUNDAY's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Prayer for attorney's fees is stricken without leave to amend. Motion is otherwise denied. Prevailing party to prepare order approved as to form. Judge Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT GREGORY T. SUNDAY and BEULAH SUNDAY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to (1) the second cause of action for Plaintiff to allege all elements of this cause of action; (2) third and fourth causes of action to re-allege as remedy and/or as a part of cognizable cause of action; (3) sixth cause of action to re-allege as separate cognizable causes of action. Demurrer is overruled as to the fifth cause of action. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges terms of the agreement as memorialized in the joint application for lot adjustment. Prevailing party to prepare order approved as to form. Judge Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
